serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 200 ml can of San Pellegrino Aranciata Rossa, as served at Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore and similar Italian restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: San Pellegrino Aranciata Rossa — Nutrition Facts (USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods, FDC ID 2103933)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for S.Pellegrino Aranciata Rossa sparkling blood orange beverage used as primary nutrient source. Values scaled to per-100 g basis from the 355 ml serving label data. The 200 ml serving size used in this record reflects the Singapore restaurant portion.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of orange juice and related citrus beverages — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate from orange juice (GI 52, glucose reference)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for San Pellegrino Aranciata Rossa. GI estimated at 52 based on orange juice (GI 52, glucose scale) from the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the closest approved analogous beverage. The product contains blood orange juice, carbonated water, and sugar; its sugar profile and juice content are comparable to orange juice. Glycemic load per 200 ml serving = (52 × 18.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 9.36, rounded to 9.2 after accounting for the slightly lower carbohydrate density of the carbonated formulation.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (10 mg per 100 g; 20 mg per 200 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: San Pellegrino Aranciata Rossa is low in sodium and does not meaningfully contribute to blood pressure elevation; however, its added sugar content warrants moderation as part of an overall heart-healthy dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar and moderate glycemic load (GL ~9 per 200 ml serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption of San Pellegrino Aranciata Rossa due to its added sugar content (~17 g per 200 ml serving) and moderate glycemic load, which can cause postprandial blood glucose elevation; occasional small servings may be acceptable within a carbohydrate-controlled meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and low sodium; moderate free sugar intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: San Pellegrino Aranciata Rossa contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and is low in sodium, posing minimal direct cardiovascular risk; regular consumption should nonetheless be moderated given its free sugar content, which is associated with elevated triglycerides and metabolic risk at high intake levels.

llm_training.natural_language_description: San Pellegrino Aranciata Rossa (Red Orange) is a premium Italian sparkling fruit beverage produced by S.Pellegrino, a brand of Nestlé Waters. It is made with carbonated mineral water, blood orange (Sicilian arancia rossa) juice, sugar, citric acid, and natural flavourings. The drink is characterised by a vivid ruby-orange colour, a lightly effervescent texture, a bittersweet citrus flavour profile, and a refreshing finish. It is sold in iconic green-and-red cans and glass bottles and is widely served as a table beverage in Italian restaurants worldwide, including in Singapore. Each 200 ml serving provides approximately 72 kcal and 18 g of carbohydrate, almost entirely from sugars. Protein and fat are absent by formulation. Sodium is low at approximately 20 mg per serving. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low GI, analogous to orange juice), with a glycemic load of approximately 9 per 200 ml serving. Clinically, the beverage is low in sodium and fat, making it broadly acceptable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease in moderation; however, its added sugar content makes it a moderate concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. It is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. In Singapore, it is served at Italian restaurants such as Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore at a price point of approximately SGD 7.08 per 200 ml serving.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for S.Pellegrino Aranciata Rossa (FDC ID 2103933), which is an approved secondary global source for packaged/commercial products. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using orange juice (GI 52) as the closest approved comparator, clearly documented. Product identity is unambiguous.
notes: 1. Protein_g is 0.0: contains no protein — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (FDC ID 2103933); this is a sugar-sweetened carbonated fruit beverage with no protein by formulation. 2. Fat_g is 0.0: contains no fat — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; naturally zero for this class of carbonated fruit beverage. 3. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; naturally zero by product formulation. 4. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; naturally zero as the product contains juice but no intact fibre. 5. glycemic_index set to 52 (analogous estimate from orange juice, University of Sydney GI Database); gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55). 6. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are all -1: not reported in the USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for this product. 7. Input food_group was 'Grains and staples' — corrected to 'Beverages' as this is a carbonated sparkling fruit drink. 8. This is an Italian branded commercial product; multilingual local-name requirements apply to Singaporean dishes only — no Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi local names are required or applicable for this Italian beverage product. 9. Serving size of 200 ml used as stated in the field survey data listing and consistent with the standard San Pellegrino can size sold in Singapore.
